toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to run the tests and collect a crash dump if the test host crashes. 

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected by setting environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` - enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` - full dump (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` - where to write the dump

Let me run the tests with these environment variables set.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-e46d38463a044335bbdf1b4739c7e8eb && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/tmp/sv-e46d38463a044335bbdf1b4739c7e8eb/crashdump_%p.dmp \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump collection enabled", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I ran:

```
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=./crashdump_%p.dmp
dotnet test
```

The `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` / `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` / `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` environment variables instruct the .NET runtime to write a full crash dump to `crashdump_<pid>.dmp` if the test host process crashes. No crash occurred this time, so no dump was generated.
